reasoning: The Lean formalization matches the informal statement. P as `Finset (Finset (Fin n))` captures "collection of subsets of {1,...,n}" (any such collection is automatically finite). `P ≠ ⊥` matches nonemptiness via `bot_eq_empty`. Conditions (i) and (ii) are faithfully encoded, including `T ⊂ S` as strict subset and `card T + 1 = card S` for "exactly one fewer element". The function `f : Finset (Fin n) → ℝ` is extended to all finsets, but since all constraints and the conclusion only reference `f` on P (closed under ∪/∩ by (i)) and on `⊥` (which is forced to be in P by iterating (ii)), this is logically equivalent to having `f : P → ℝ`. The conclusion `∃ r : Fin n → ℝ, ∀ S ∈ P, f S = ∑ i ∈ S, r i` matches, and the whole being asserted iff `True` (the solution) correctly encodes the affirmative answer.
